message:
chore(beads): assign OliveGlacier to E2E scripts expansion task

Update issue tracker: assign agent OliveGlacier to bd-26l3 (E2E scripts
expansion + log enrichment).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -231,7 +231,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-263w","title":"[EPIC-INFRA] Hierarchical Timer Wheel","description":"## Overview

Implement a hierarchical timer wheel for O(1) timer operations, replacing naive linear timer storage. This is a performance-critical optimization for workloads with many concurrent timers.

## Strategic Value

**Problem Solved**: Current timer implementations (if using Vec or BTreeMap) have O(n) or O(log n) cost for timer operations. Servers handling thousands of connections each with timeouts need O(1) operations.

**Why Hierarchical**: Simple timer wheels have limited range. Hierarchical wheels (like Linux kernel) provide O(1) insert/cancel/expire across wide time ranges (milliseconds to hours).

**Production Necessity**: Any serious async runtime needs efficient timer management. This is foundational for production use.

## Algorithm Overview

### Timer Wheel Structure
```
Level 0: 256 slots, 1ms resolution (covers 256ms)
Level 1: 64 slots, 256ms resolution (covers 16.4 seconds)  
Level 2: 64 slots, 16.4s resolution (covers 17.5 minutes)
Level 3: 64 slots, 17.5min resolution (covers 18.6 hours)
```

### Operations
- **Insert**: Hash deadline to appropriate level/slot, O(1)
- **Cancel**: Remove from linked list at slot, O(1) with handle
- **Tick**: Process Level 0, cascade higher levels periodically

### Cascading
When Level 0 wraps, cascade Level 1 slot into Level 0. This amortizes the cost of long-duration timers.

## Design Considerations

### Memory Layout
- Intrusive linked lists for zero-allocation in steady state
- Timer handles store list pointers for O(1) cancel
- Cache-friendly slot arrays

### Precision Trade-offs
- 1ms base resolution is sufficient for network timeouts
- Sub-millisecond needs separate approach (spin loops, etc.)
- Configuration allows tuning resolution vs. memory

### Integration
- Replace current timer storage in time module
- Maintain same API (sleep, timeout, Delay)
- Lab runtime gets separate virtual time wheel

## Acceptance Criteria

1. O(1) insert, cancel, and per-tick expire operations
2. Supports at least 24-hour timer range
3. Memory overhead < 1KB for wheel structure
4. Benchmark shows 10x improvement over BTreeMap for 10K timers
5. No changes to public timer API

## Dependencies

- Standalone module, no major dependencies
- Benefits from good integration test coverage

## Priority Rationale

Ranked #7 because while important for production performance, it is primarily an internal optimization. Users benefit indirectly through better performance, but it does not add new capabilities.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T18:02:27.423805196Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-30T04:31:56.960796836Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T04:31:56.960713191Z","close_reason":"All 4 sub-tasks closed: gt1c (Lab virtual time wheel), jrrw (overflow and coalescing), h7as (comprehensive test suite), uyqb (benchmarks). HierarchicalTimerWheel in src/time/intrusive_wheel.rs, VirtualTimerWheel in src/lab/virtual_time_wheel.rs, TimerWheel in src/time/wheel.rs. O(1) operations, hierarchical 4-level design, tests passing, benchmarks complete.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-263w","depends_on_id":"bd-gt1c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:01:52Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-263w","depends_on_id":"bd-h7as","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:01:52Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-263w","depends_on_id":"bd-jrrw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:01:52Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-263w","depends_on_id":"bd-uyqb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:01:52Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-26fo","title":"[ALIEN][PLAN] Extractor: cost model + deterministic best-plan selection","description":"## Purpose

Choose an optimized representative from the e-graph.

## Cost model (initial)

- allocations (estimated)
- number of cancel checkpoints
- obligation pressure
- critical path (Foata depth proxy)

All costs must be deterministic.

## Acceptance criteria

- Extraction is deterministic.
- Output plan is accompanied by a derivation certificate (see P6).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:23.615991867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:58:49.621473368Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T06:58:49.621368273Z","close_reason":"Extractor cost model + deterministic extraction in place; fixed tests (j2_inner, CertificateVersion::from_number)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","performance","plan"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26fo","depends_on_id":"bd-1cac","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-26fo","depends_on_id":"bd-1cir","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-26fo","depends_on_id":"bd-2de4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-26fo","depends_on_id":"bd-vve1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-26gm","title":"[AA][DPOR] Deterministic fuzzing + invariant oracles","description":"## Goal
Add deterministic fuzzing and invariant oracles for structured concurrency.

## Why
We need systematic exploration beyond unit tests with reproducible seeds.

## Deliverables
- Fuzz harness for scheduling decisions.
- Invariant oracles (no leaks, region close ⇒ quiescence).
- Seed triage + minimization.

## Acceptance
- Fuzzer finds known past bugs and produces stable repros.
- Oracles fail fast with deterministic seeds.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:09:04.846490797Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:56:54.094868800Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:56:54.094785044Z","close_reason":"Added src/lab/fuzz.rs: deterministic fuzz harness with FuzzConfig, FuzzHarness, FuzzReport, FuzzFinding. Seed-driven exploration with invariant oracle checking (obligation leak, task leak, actor leak, quiescence, futurelock). Seed minimization via bit-flips and offset search. fuzz_quick() convenience function. 5 tests. Exported in lab/mod.rs.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26gm","depends_on_id":"bd-101w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:09:04.853705350Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-26gm","depends_on_id":"bd-1b84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:11:57.377163826Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-26l3","title":"[TEST] E2E scripts expansion + log enrichment","description":"Goal: comprehensive E2E scripts with detailed, deterministic logging and failure artifacts.

Detailed checklist:
- For each subsystem (net/websocket/http/h2, messaging, transport, db, distributed), ensure an E2E script exists.
- Use the standardized harness/logging schema from bd-12vq.
- Add structured log sections, seed info, and final summary.
- Capture artifacts on failure (trace files, repro seeds, failing inputs).
- Ensure scripts are hermetic and do not mutate code or rely on ambient state.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T15:39:23.657729379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:36:49.317864774Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","scripts","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26l3","depends_on_id":"bd-12vq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T15:40:16.506037662Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-26l3","depends_on_id":"bd-1ex7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T15:53:12.550185210Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-26l3","depends_on_id":"bd-2alu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T15:40:16.365215088Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-26l3","depends_on_id":"bd-2c7u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T15:39:23.680556616Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-26l3","depends_on_id":"bd-76y5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T15:53:03.310736271Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-26v8","title":"[ALIEN][PLAN] Semantics spec: trace-equivalence + side-condition schema","description":"## Purpose

Define what it means for two Plan IR programs to be "the same" in Asupersync, and define the schema that makes rewrite rules checkable.

This is the foundation for:
- e-graph search (we can only merge nodes if we know the equivalence notion)
- certificates + verifier (what exactly is being certified?)
- lab equivalence oracle (how we test equivalence deterministically)

## Equivalence notion (independence-aware, not schedule-identical)

We target observational equivalence up to the runtime's trace semantics.

Two plans P and Q are equivalent if, under the same deterministic lab configuration (seed, policies, budgets):
- they produce the same terminal Outcome (including CancelReason / severity lattice position)
- they satisfy the same safety invariants (no obligation leaks, region close => quiescence, losers drained)
- their executions are equivalent up to allowed commutations of independent events (Mazurkiewicz / trace-monoid equivalence)

We do NOT require identical step-by-step schedules.

Operationally, the lab equivalence oracle will compare:
- canonicalized trace fingerprints (Foata / independence-normalized)
- plus explicit observable state projections (obligation ledger summaries, region tree quiescence)

## Allowed law families (rewrite preconditions)

Every rewrite rule must declare which laws it relies on, in machine-checkable form.

- algebraic laws for combinators (examples):
  - monoid: associativity of join, identity elements
  - semiring-like: distributivity or absorption, if applicable

- concurrency / trace laws:
  - commutation only when independence is proven
  - loser-drain laws for race

- cancellation protocol laws:
  - request -> drain -> finalize (idempotent)
  - masking rules (where cancellation can/cannot be observed)

- obligation laws:
  - linearity: permits/acks/leases must be resolved
  - no implicit drops

- budget laws:
  - meet/propagation laws for Budget semiring
  - deadline safety: rewrites must not weaken deadlines or increase required poll quota beyond declared bound

## Side-condition schema (what the analysis must prove)

Each rewrite step must carry a side-condition object that can be checked by a small verifier:
- independence conditions for commutations
- obligation-flow safety conditions (no new leak paths)
- cancellation safety conditions (loser drain preserved, cancel responsiveness not worsened beyond bound)
- budget monotonicity conditions
- determinism conditions (no iteration-order dependence introduced)

## Certificate/verifier contract (ties into bd-2jck)

A certificate is a sequence of rewrite steps plus their side conditions, with stable hashing.

The verifier must:
- validate each step matches a known rule schema
- validate all referenced side conditions (via analysis summaries or explicit witnesses)
- validate deterministic hashing / ordering constraints

## Acceptance criteria

- Provides an unambiguous equivalence target for tests and certificates.
- Enumerates law families and side-condition schema explicitly.
- Defines what data the verifier needs and what the lab oracle must compare.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:21.783526599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:31:17.556348604Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:31:17.556249329Z","close_reason":"Added trace-equivalence + side-condition schema to formal semantics docs","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","plan","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26v8","depends_on_id":"bd-1cir","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:23:17Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-274qo","title":"Perf Regression CI Gates","description":"Add perf regression gating in CI: fail when p95 latency/throughput regressions exceed thresholds.
Acceptance: CI job + baseline artifacts + documented override process for intentional regressions, plus structured CI logs capturing environment/config/variance and a smoke test that verifies gate behavior on synthetic regressions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:06:47.095320114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:40:38.707853697Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-274qo","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeb7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:14:03.781431274Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-274qo","depends_on_id":"bd-4bfy4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:06:47.108815765Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-275by","title":"Obligation Lifecycle E2E Tests + Logging","description":"End-to-end tests that exercise two-phase channels, obligation tokens, and region close with structured tracing.
